AeroNav
Copilot Memory Live layers Roadmap
Get updates

Privacy by design

Privacy

Last updated July 8, 2026. AeroNav is designed so your personal sky history feels like it belongs to you, not an ad network.

What AeroNav May Use

AeroNav may use flight queries, selected aircraft, favorite routes, airport searches, notification choices, app activity, and device settings to power live tracking, the AI copilot, alerts, streaks, ranks, and your personal logbook.

Voice and Search

Voice prompts and typed searches are used to understand requests such as tracking a friend's flight from Dubai to Seattle. The copilot should verify live data before answering and should say when it cannot know something confidently.

Private Memory

The roadmap prioritizes private, on-device history wherever practical. Personal logbook entries, lifetime stats, favorite airlines, rare-aircraft moments, and streaks are intended to be controlled by you.

No Ads

AeroNav is positioned as free and ad-free. The goal is not to sell attention, but to create a calmer flight-tracking experience for aviation lovers.

Your Choices

  • You can choose which flights, airports, routes, and alerts matter to you.
  • You can contact AeroNav about privacy questions or data concerns.
  • If features change, this page should be updated to describe the new behavior clearly.

Contact

Privacy questions can be sent to aeronav3d@gmail.com.

AeroNav Other apps show you the sky. AeroNav remembers yours.

Free, ad-free, and built with private on-device history for aviation lovers.
